speaker: MALCOLM
text: Cousins, I hope the days are near at hand

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That chambers will be safe.

speaker: MENTEITH
text: We doubt it nothing.

speaker: SIWARD
text: What wood is this before us?

speaker: MENTEITH
text: The Wood of Birnam.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Let every soldier hew him down a bough

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And bear ’t before him. Thereby shall we shadow

speaker: MALCOLM
text: The numbers of our host and make discovery

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Err in report of us.

speaker: SOLDIER
text: It shall be done.

speaker: SIWARD
text: We learn no other but the confident tyrant

speaker: SIWARD
text: Keeps still in Dunsinane and will endure

speaker: SIWARD
text: Our setting down before ’t.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: ’Tis his main hope;

speaker: MALCOLM
text: For, where there is advantage to be given,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Both more and less have given him the revolt,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And none serve with him but constrainèd things

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Whose hearts are absent too.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Let our just censures

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Attend the true event, and put we on

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Industrious soldiership.

speaker: SIWARD
text: The time approaches

speaker: SIWARD
text: That will with due decision make us know

speaker: SIWARD
text: What we shall say we have and what we owe.

speaker: SIWARD
text: Thoughts speculative their unsure hopes relate,

speaker: SIWARD
text: But certain issue strokes must arbitrate;

speaker: SIWARD
text: Towards which, advance the war.
